maml:description.maml:para: Validate pending domains.

Immediately validates the domains using the DNS CNAME, DNS TXT, and HTTP methods. Use this operation when you've already updated your DNS or HTTP server and want to skip the standard queue to start the validation now.
Name of the domain.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Preferred validation method for instant validation of a domain, either DNS_CNAME, DNS_TXT, or HTTP.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Preferred validation method for instant validation of a domain, either DNS_CNAME, DNS_TXT, or HTTP.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Invalidate domains.
Invalidates the specified domains. When you invalidate a domain, Akamai doesn't recognize you as its owner. The domain is then automatically deleted as part of a cleanup procedure.
Name of the domain.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Search for domains.
Returns the status of specified domains. For any nonexistent domains, the API returns the closest matching domain status.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Whether to return a detailed response.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Whether to return a detailed response.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Whether to return a detailed response.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Get a domain.
Lists all domains in Domain Ownership Manager, or returns a specific domain by its DomainName.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Whether to return domain status history.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Page index starting with 1. By default, `1`.
Number of records to fetch on a page. By default, `1000`.
Whether to return a paginated response.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Whether to return domain status history.
Page index starting with 1. By default, `1`.
Number of records to fetch on a page. By default, `1000`.
Whether to return a paginated response.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Add domains.

Add domains to validate. For each domain you create DomainName you provide you must also provide a ValidationScope of either 'HOST', 'WILDCARD' or 'DOMAIN'. If you provide more than one entry for DomainName, the used value for ValidationScope will be the one at the same position in the provided list. For example, the first DomainName will be paired with the first ValidationScope, the second the second, and so on.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Batch-delete domains.

Deletes the specified domains. You can only delete domains that are invalidated or have expired tokens. When you delete domains, Akamai doesn't recognize you as their owner. To revalidate deleted domains, submit them for validation again.
Name of the domain.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Name of the domain.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Validation scope of the domain. For `HOST`, the scope is only the exactly specified domain. For `WILDCARD`, the scope covers any hostname within one subdomain level. For `DOMAIN`, the scope covers any hostnames under the domain, regardless of the level of subdomains. Read more about validation scopes (ref:api-workflow).
